implemented_advanced_coverage.per_rule_budget_exhaustion: partial: enforced for semantic/business rules; schema_rule_id failures continue to global budget to match visible core scenarios

elixir_owns: JSON decoding and HTTP response assembly
gleam_elixir_boundary_shape.boundary_notes: Elixir loads the nested Gleam project's BEAM modules from structured_core/build when available and has identical Elixir fallback decisions so the HTTP app can still start if the Gleam build artifacts are absent.

isolation_behavior.shape: Application supervises Registry plus DynamicSupervisor pairs for model runtimes and session runtimes. Chat completions execute through a session runtime keyed by synthetic model and session id.
isolation_behavior.native_test: A deliberately crashed session process is replaced and does not break another session under the same model.
limitations: The structured-output loop is still synchronous inside the session process.
There is no per-model pool sizing or queue-depth telemetry yet.
Model runtime currently records process boundary only; route graph state remains in persistent config.

risks: A single session process serializes work for that session; long provider calls can delay later requests in the same session.
Unbounded dynamic session creation could grow process counts without idle reaping.
Repeated crash loops are supervised but not rate-limited beyond DynamicSupervisor defaults.
Cold start is low for local BEAM processes but still creates model/session processes on first use.
sidecar_backpressure_risks.next_iteration: Add per-model/session child specs with idle timeout, bounded request queues, telemetry for queue length and restarts, and pool or task handoff for provider calls that should not serialize unrelated work.

known_limitations: Only the JSON-schema subset used by the bakeoff visible fixtures is implemented: object type, required fields, string minLength/enum, number min/max, arrays of strings, and additionalProperties false.
The canned_sequence provider is test-config only and consumes outputs per request rather than maintaining shared provider state.
Per-rule exhaustion is applied to semantic_validation events; repeated json_syntax/schema_validation events use the global attempt budget.
Gleam owns pure decision functions but not the full schema validator because JSON interop and decode work remained faster and clearer in Elixir inside the timebox.
setup_friction: mise.toml had to be trusted before local tools could run.
Gleam was not configured initially; installed gleam 1.14.0 through mise after gleam_stdlib required >= 1.14.0.
Mix dependencies were not fetched in the worktree and required mix deps.get before Elixir tests could run.

checks_run.command: cd backends/elixir-ingary/structured_core && mise exec -- gleam format --check src test && mise exec -- gleam test
cd backends/elixir-ingary && mise exec -- mix format --check-formatted && mise exec -- mix test

mutation_check.change: Temporarily weakened confidence semantic validation from value >= configured gte to value >= 0.0.
mutation_check.command: cd backends/elixir-ingary && mise exec -- mix test --seed 1 test/elixir_ingary_test.exs:293
mutation_check.observed_failure: Generated low-confidence property failed because a confidence 0.0 response completed instead of exhausting the semantic rule budget.

correctness: Receipts include final status, selected schema, parsed output on success, attempt count, ordered guard events, rule ids, guard types, actions, exhausted rule id, model id/version, session id, and failure domain.
Core visible scenarios and generated confidence properties are translated into native ExUnit tests.
architecture: The Elixir runtime shell remains responsible for HTTP, config, supervision, and receipts.
Gleam's current value is modest but real for status/budget ADTs; a fuller evaluator would move schema/config normalization into Gleam once interop shape settles.
security: No credentials, private endpoints, prompts, or provider tokens were added.
Provider raw output is only fixture-controlled canned JSON in tests; receipts store parsed structured output but do not log raw user prompts or credentials.
test_quality: Tests assert public HTTP behavior and receipt shape rather than private functions.
Mutation check proved the semantic confidence property can fail for the intended behavior.
